Mitosis is a type of cell division that results in the production of somatic cells, which are all the cells in the body except for reproductive cells. It involves the duplication and subsequent division of chromosomes, ensuring that each resulting daughter cell receives an identical set of genetic material. This process maintains the genetic consistency of the species.  In contrast, meiosis is a specialized form of cell division that specifically produces gametes (eggs or sperm) in sexually reproducing organisms. Meiosis involves two rounds of genetic material separation, leading to four genetically diverse haploid cells
